messages.text: Hi, I was charged twice this month for my Pro plan. Can you check?
Hi Jordan! I'm sorry about that. Let me pull up your billing history right now.
I can see the duplicate charge. It looks like a processing error on our end. I've initiated a refund for the extra $29. You should see it in 3-5 business days.
That was fast! Thank you so much.
Is there a way to export my tasks to a CSV file? I need to share a project list with my team who doesn't use Orbit.
Hi Priya, thanks for reaching out! CSV export is available on the Pro plan. On the Free plan, you can copy-paste from the table view as a workaround. Would you like to learn more about upgrading?
Hmm, I was hoping it would be in the free tier. I'll think about upgrading. Thanks.
The Gantt chart view crashes every time I try to add a dependency between tasks. Started happening after your update last night.
Sorry about that, Marcus. Can you tell me which browser you're using and roughly how many tasks are in the project?
Chrome latest version. The project has about 85 tasks.

I've reproduced the issue on our end. This appears to be a bug from last night's release. I'm escalating this to our engineering team as a priority fix. In the meantime, you can add dependencies from the task detail view as a workaround.
OK, the workaround helps. Please let me know when it's fixed.
Will do! I've added you to the notification list for this fix. You'll get an email as soon as it's deployed.
I just upgraded to Pro and I'm trying to set up automations. How do I create a rule that automatically moves a task to 'In Review' when all subtasks are completed?

Great question, Samira! Here's how to set that up:
1. Go to Project Settings > Automations
2. Click 'New Rule'
3. Set trigger: 'All subtasks completed'
4. Set action: 'Move task to In Review'
5. Save and enable

This will apply to all new tasks. Want me to walk you through setting it up for existing tasks too?
Perfect, that worked! Yes, please show me how to apply it retroactively.

For existing tasks, go to the automation you just created, click the three dots menu, and select 'Run on existing tasks'. It will scan your project and apply the rule to any tasks that already meet the criteria.
I signed up for the free plan but I'm being charged $12/month? I never agreed to this.
Hi Devon, let me look into this. Can you confirm the email on your account?
d.park.designs@email.com
I see that your account started a Pro trial on Oct 5, and it converted to a paid plan when the trial ended on Oct 19. We sent a reminder email 3 days before conversion.
I never saw that email. This feels really sneaky. I want a refund and to cancel immediately.

I understand your frustration. I've cancelled your Pro subscription effective immediately and downgraded you to Free. For the refund, I'll need to escalate to our billing team — they typically process these within 48 hours.
This should never have happened in the first place. Your trial-to-paid conversion is deceptive.
Several team members report that tasks assigned to them disappear from their 'My Tasks' view after about an hour. They have to refresh the page to see them again. This started 2 days ago.

Hi Aisha, thank you for the detailed report. We've identified a caching issue affecting the My Tasks view for Team plan accounts. Our engineering team is working on a fix. In the meantime, the issue only affects the filtered view — tasks are still assigned correctly in the full project view.
Thanks for confirming it's a known issue. When do you expect the fix?
Does Orbit support Slack integration yet? I saw it mentioned on your roadmap last quarter.
Hi Leo! Slack integration is currently in beta. You can enable it from Settings > Integrations > Slack (Beta). It supports task notifications and creating tasks from Slack messages.
Oh nice, I didn't realize it was already there. Can it also update task status from Slack?
Not yet — status updates from Slack are planned for the next release. Right now it's notifications and task creation only.
Good enough for now. Thanks!

I'm new to Orbit and I'm managing a team of 8 designers. What's the best way to set up our projects so everyone can see their tasks without seeing everything else? We do client work and need some privacy between projects.

Welcome to Orbit, Rachel! For your use case, I'd recommend:
1. Create a separate project per client
2. Use the 'Members' tab on each project to control who sees what
3. On the Free plan, all members can see all projects — for per-project privacy, you'd need the Team plan

Would you like me to walk you through the setup?
Ah, so we need Team plan for privacy. Good to know. Let me discuss with my lead. Thanks for the clear explanation.
We lost data. I'm not kidding. An entire project with 60+ tasks just disappeared. It's not in archive, it's not in trash. Gone.
Tyler, I'm really sorry to hear this. Let me escalate this immediately. Can you give me the project name and roughly when you last saw it?
Project name was 'Q4 Product Launch'. I was in it this morning at 8am. By noon it was gone. 3 weeks of planning — gone.

I've flagged this as critical with our engineering team. I can see in our logs that the project exists in our backup system. We should be able to restore it, but I need to coordinate with engineering. I'll stay on this and update you within 2 hours.
2 hours?? My team has a deadline TOMORROW. This is completely unacceptable.
I completely understand the urgency. I've escalated this to our on-call engineer and marked it as P0. I'll message you the moment we have an update, which should be well within the 2-hour window.
We're a nonprofit and I saw that you offer discounted pricing. How do we apply for that?

Hi Nina! Yes, we offer 50% off for registered nonprofits. To apply:
1. Go to Settings > Billing > Discounts
2. Select 'Nonprofit'
3. Upload your 501(c)(3) or equivalent documentation
4. We'll review within 2 business days

The discount applies to all paid plans.
Wonderful! Just submitted the application. Thanks for making this easy.
How do I set up a project template? We start the same type of project every month and I'm tired of recreating the same 30 tasks each time.

Great question, Brandon! Here's the fastest way:
1. Open the project you want to use as a template
2. Click the three dots menu > 'Save as Template'
3. Name it and choose which elements to include (tasks, subtasks, descriptions, assignments)
4. Next time, create a new project and select 'From Template'

The template will copy everything except dates — you set new dates when you create the project.
That's exactly what I needed. Can I share templates with other team members?
Yes! On the Team plan, templates are shared with everyone in your workspace by default. You can also mark them as 'Personal' if you want to keep them private.
Perfect. This is going to save us hours every month. Thanks!
I need to track time spent on tasks. Does Orbit have built-in time tracking or do I need a separate tool?

Hi Yuki! Time tracking is on our roadmap but not available natively yet. Most of our users integrate with tools like Clockify or Toggl. We have a guide on setting that up: [link]. Would that work for your needs?
Disappointing but thanks. I'll check out Toggl. Any ETA on native time tracking?
The mobile app on Android crashes whenever I try to open a task with more than 5 attachments. iPhone works fine.
Thanks for reporting this, Emma. What Android version and device are you using?
Pixel 8, Android 15.

I've confirmed this is a known issue with Android 15 specifically. A fix is in our next mobile release (v3.4.2), expected next week. For now, you can view attachment-heavy tasks in the mobile browser as a workaround.
OK, thanks for the quick response. Looking forward to the fix.
We need to add 5 more seats to our Team plan. Can we do this mid-cycle or do we have to wait for renewal?

Hi Carlos! You can add seats anytime. Go to Settings > Billing > Manage Seats. The new seats are prorated for the rest of your billing cycle. So if you're halfway through the month, you'll pay half-price for each new seat this month, then full price next month.
Done! Added 5 seats. Thanks for the clear explanation on prorating.
Is there a way to see all tasks due this week across all my projects in one view?
Yes! Click on 'My Work' in the left sidebar. It shows all tasks assigned to you across every project, and you can filter by 'Due This Week'. You can also switch between list and calendar views there.
Oh wow, I've been using Orbit for 2 months and didn't know about My Work. This changes everything.
It's one of those hidden gems! You can also pin it to the top of your sidebar so it's always one click away.
Done. You just made my week. Thank you!
The PDF export feature is completely broken. I've tried 4 times and every export comes out with scrambled formatting — headers are in the wrong place, tables are cut off, and images are missing.
Hi Omar, sorry about this. Could you share a screenshot of what the PDF looks like? Also, which project are you trying to export?
I attached 3 screenshots. This is embarrassing — I needed to send this to a client yesterday and had to manually recreate the report in Google Docs instead.

I can see the issue clearly in your screenshots. I've escalated this to our engineering team with your examples. I know this doesn't help with yesterday's deadline, and I'm sorry about that. We're treating this as high priority.
We need to set up role-based permissions. Our interns should only be able to view and comment, not edit or delete tasks. Is this possible?
Hi Sophie! On the Team plan, you have 3 permission roles: Admin, Member, and Guest. Guests can view and comment but can't edit or delete. You'd invite interns as Guests.
But we also need a 'Contributor' role — someone who can edit tasks assigned to them but not other people's tasks. Guest is too restrictive.
That's a great point. Custom roles aren't available yet — it's on our roadmap for Q1. For now, the workaround is to use separate projects with different member lists.
That's clunky but I get it. Please prioritize custom roles — it's a blocker for larger teams.
I want to set up a weekly status report that automatically pulls data from my projects. Is there a way to automate this?

Yes! Go to Reports > Scheduled Reports > Create New. You can choose:
- Which projects to include
- What metrics (tasks completed, overdue, created)
- Frequency (weekly, biweekly, monthly)
- Recipients (your team, your manager, or anyone via email)

The report auto-generates and sends every week.
This is exactly what I needed. My manager asks for status every Monday and I spend 30 minutes compiling it. Not anymore!
Glad to help! Pro tip: you can also include a custom note at the top of each report for context.
I want to upgrade to Pro but I'm worried about losing my data during the transition. Will my projects and tasks transfer over?
Hi Diana! Great news — upgrading is seamless. All your projects, tasks, files, and history stay exactly where they are. You just gain access to Pro features instantly. Nothing gets deleted or moved.
That's reassuring. Just upgraded. Everything looks the same plus the new features. Perfect.
Notifications are completely broken for our team. Nobody is getting email notifications for task assignments or due date reminders. This has been going on for 3 days.
I'm sorry to hear that, Alex. Let me check the notification system status.

I can confirm there's been a notification delivery delay affecting some Team accounts. Our infrastructure team is working on it. Notifications are queued and will be delivered, but with significant delay (up to 6 hours).
6 hours?? That makes notifications useless. People are missing deadlines because of this. We pay for the Team plan specifically for reliable team features.

You're absolutely right, and I understand the impact. I've escalated this to P1 priority. As a temporary measure, you can enable in-app push notifications which are working normally. I'll personally follow up with you once the email notifications are fully restored.
I'm running a 6-month project and need to track milestones. What's the best way to do this in Orbit?

Hi Mika! For milestones in a long project, I'd recommend:
1. Create a 'Milestones' section at the top of your project
2. Use the Gantt view to set milestone dates as zero-duration tasks
3. Set up milestone notifications (Settings > Notifications > Milestones)
4. Use the Timeline view to see all milestones across the 6 months

The Timeline view is especially useful for presenting to stakeholders.
The zero-duration task trick for milestones is clever. Set it all up and it looks great on the Gantt chart. Thank you!
We need SSO with Okta. Is this supported? Our IT team won't approve the tool without it.
SSO with Okta is available on the Enterprise plan. It supports SAML 2.0 and SCIM for user provisioning. Would you like me to connect you with our sales team to discuss Enterprise pricing?
How much more is Enterprise vs Team?

Enterprise pricing is custom based on team size and needs. I can have our sales team send you a quote within 24 hours. They can also set up a trial of Enterprise features for your IT team to evaluate.
OK, please have them reach out. But honestly, SSO should be in the Team plan. Every competitor includes it.
I tried importing my Trello board and it only brought over 20 of my 85 cards. The rest are just missing. No error message.

Hi Chris, sorry about that. The Free plan import has a limit of 20 tasks. Unfortunately this wasn't clearly communicated during the import flow — I've flagged that as a UX issue. The full import (unlimited tasks) is available on Pro and Team plans.
That's pretty frustrating to find out after the fact. You should warn people BEFORE they start the import, not after they've already lost data structure.
I manage 3 client projects in Orbit. Is there a way to see a portfolio view — like high-level status of all projects on one screen?

Yes! On Pro, go to the main dashboard and click 'Portfolio View' at the top. It shows all your projects with:
- Overall progress percentage
- Tasks on track vs overdue
- Next milestone for each project
- A health indicator (green/yellow/red)

You can also filter by tag or team member.
This is incredible. I've been switching between 3 project tabs. This one view gives me everything I need for my Monday client check-ins.
Glad it helps! You can also share the portfolio view as a read-only link with clients if you want to give them visibility without full access.
We want to switch from monthly to annual billing. Can this be done mid-subscription?

Hi Ryan! Yes, you can switch to annual billing anytime. Go to Settings > Billing > Change Plan. When you switch:
- You'll get 2 months free (annual = 10 months price for 12 months)
- Your remaining monthly balance is credited toward the annual price
- The change takes effect immediately
Done. The credit calculation was clear and the savings are significant. Thanks!
The drag-and-drop on the board view is completely broken in Firefox. Tasks jump to random positions when I try to move them.
Sorry about that, Ingrid. We have a known issue with Firefox and our drag-and-drop library. Which Firefox version are you on?
Firefox 131.

Thanks. This is a known issue specific to Firefox 131+. A fix is in testing and should ship within the week. In the meantime, you can reorder tasks using the right-click menu > 'Move to' option, or use Chrome/Edge where drag-and-drop works normally.
I'd rather not switch browsers but I'll use the menu option for now. Please fix this soon.
I need to add custom fields to my tasks. For example, a 'Client Name' field and a 'Priority Score' number field. Can Orbit do this?

Hi Daniel! Custom fields are available on the Pro plan and above. You can create text, number, dropdown, date, and checkbox field types. On the Free plan, a common workaround is using tags for categories like client names.
Tags don't really cut it for numeric scores. I'll look into upgrading. Thanks.
Our team has 25 people. What's the best way to organize projects — one workspace with everything, or separate workspaces per department?

For a team of 25, I'd recommend one workspace with project-level organization:
- Use project groups for departments (Engineering, Marketing, Sales, etc.)
- Use tags for cross-department themes
- Control access at the project level

Separate workspaces make it hard to see cross-team dependencies and you can't share templates between them.
That makes sense. We were leaning toward separate workspaces but the cross-team visibility point is important. We'll do one workspace with groups.
Great choice! One more tip: use the 'Department' custom field on projects. It makes filtering and reporting by department super easy in the portfolio view.
The search function returns results from archived projects that I can't even access anymore. When I click them, I get a 'Project not found' error. This happens every single time I search.
Hi Kevin, thanks for reporting this. I can reproduce the issue. The search index includes archived projects but the access check doesn't account for the archive status. I've filed this as a bug.
Filed as a bug is fine, but can you at least give me a filter to exclude archived results? This makes search basically unusable.
We need to remove 3 team members who left the company. Will we get credit for the unused portion of their seats?

Hi Fatima! Yes, when you remove seats, the unused portion is automatically credited to your account. Go to Settings > Team > Members, deactivate the users, then go to Billing > Manage Seats to reduce the seat count. The credit applies to your next invoice.
One question — when I deactivate a user, what happens to their assigned tasks?

Great question! Their tasks stay in the projects but become 'unassigned'. You can use the filter 'Unassigned tasks' to find and reassign them. The deactivated user's comments and history are preserved.
Perfect, that's exactly what I needed to know. Done — 3 users deactivated and seats reduced. Thanks!
Can Orbit integrate with our CRM? We use a tool called PipelineFlow and want task updates to sync with our deal stages.

Hi Anton! We don't have a native PipelineFlow integration, but you can connect them via our API or through Zapier. Common setups include:
- When a deal moves to 'Closed Won' in PipelineFlow, auto-create an onboarding project in Orbit
- When a task is completed in Orbit, update the deal notes in PipelineFlow

Would you like our API docs or a Zapier setup guide?
Zapier would be easiest for us. Please send the guide. Thanks!
I want to use Orbit for my wedding planning. Is there a template for events or personal projects?

What a fun use case! We have a community template gallery at Templates > Browse Community. There are several event planning templates including one for weddings. It includes sections for vendors, venue, guest list, timeline, and budget tracking.
Found it! The wedding template is surprisingly detailed. Love the vendor checklist. Thanks!
Enjoy the planning! Pro tip: you can share the project with your partner or wedding planner as a Guest so they can see progress without editing.
For the THIRD time this month, the app is down. We're a team of 40 and nobody can access their tasks. This is unacceptable for a paid product. We're evaluating alternatives.

Robert, I sincerely apologize for the repeated outages. We're currently experiencing a service disruption and our team is actively working to restore access. I understand this is severely impacting your team's productivity.
It's been 2.5 hours now. I need an ETA and I need to know what you're doing to prevent this from happening again. My director is asking why we chose Orbit.

Service has been restored as of 10:15am. I've requested that our CTO prepare a post-incident report which will be shared with affected Team and Enterprise customers within 48 hours. I'll also have our account manager reach out to discuss how we can make this right.
I expect that report. And serious compensation for this month. Three outages is a pattern, not an incident.
How do I set up task dependencies? I need Task B to start only after Task A is completed.

Easy! Open Task B, scroll to the 'Dependencies' section, and click 'Add dependency'. Choose 'Blocked by' and select Task A. Now Task B will show as blocked until Task A is marked complete. The Gantt view will also show the dependency line between them.
That was simple. I see the dependency arrow in the Gantt chart now. Can I set up a chain? Like A blocks B blocks C?
Absolutely! Just add dependencies on each task in the chain. Orbit supports complex dependency chains and will even warn you if you create a circular dependency.
Love it. Our project timeline finally makes sense visually. Thank you!
We want to downgrade from Team to Pro but it says we'll lose access to team features immediately. Can we stay on Team until the end of our billing cycle?

Hi Pat, currently downgrades take effect immediately because Team features like advanced permissions and team reporting need to be disabled. However, I can schedule the downgrade for the end of your billing cycle manually on our end. Would you like me to do that?
Yes please. It's frustrating that the self-service option doesn't allow this. We paid for the full month.

Done — your Team plan will remain active until December 15 (end of current cycle), then automatically switch to Pro. I've also shared your feedback about the self-service downgrade flow with our product team.
Does Orbit have an API? We want to build a simple dashboard that pulls task data into our internal reporting tool.

Yes! We have a REST API available on Pro and above. It covers projects, tasks, users, comments, and time entries. You can find the docs at docs.orbittask.com/api. We also have a webhook system if you need real-time updates.
Great. Is there a rate limit?
Pro plan gets 1,000 requests/hour. Enterprise gets 10,000. For a dashboard that pulls data periodically, Pro limits should be more than enough.
Perfect. I'll share the docs with our developer. Thanks!
I set a due date on a task but the reminder never came. It's been 2 days past due and I only realized because I manually checked.

Hi Grace, sorry about that! On the Free plan, due date reminders are via in-app notification only (not email). Can you check if you have in-app notifications enabled? Go to Settings > Notifications > Due Date Reminders.
Ah, it was turned off by default. That seems like a bad default. Turned it on now.
We want to track sprint velocity. Is there a built-in way to see how many story points we complete per sprint?

Yes! First, enable the 'Story Points' custom field on your tasks (Project Settings > Custom Fields). Then go to Reports > Sprint Report. It shows:
- Points committed vs completed per sprint
- Velocity trend over the last 6 sprints
- Burndown chart for the current sprint

You can also export this data for your retrospectives.
This is exactly what our scrum master has been asking for. Setting it up now.
One more tip: if you set up sprint cycles (Project Settings > Sprints), Orbit will auto-calculate velocity and even predict if your current sprint is on track based on historical data.
My receipt doesn't show our company name. I need proper invoices with our business name and VAT number for accounting.

Hi Zara! You can add your company details at Settings > Billing > Invoice Settings. Enter your company name, address, and VAT number. This will apply to all future invoices and you can also regenerate past invoices with the updated info.
Found it. Updated and regenerated last month's invoice. Exactly what I needed, thank you!
Real-time collaboration is broken. When two people edit the same task description simultaneously, one person's changes get overwritten with no warning. We've lost work 3 times today.
That's a serious issue, James. Our collaboration feature should show a 'someone else is editing' indicator. Can you tell me more about when this happens — is it in the description field specifically?
Yes, specifically the description field. Comments work fine. It seems like the last person to save wins and the other person's changes vanish.

I've confirmed this with our team. The real-time sync for description fields has a bug where it falls back to 'last write wins' instead of proper conflict resolution. I'm escalating this immediately. Until it's fixed, I'd recommend having only one person edit a task description at a time, or using comments for collaborative input.
That defeats the purpose of collaboration. Please fix this urgently.
